LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT INFORMS THE PLAINTIFF AT THE HEARING THAT IT HAS NOT RECEIVED A COURTESY COPY OF THE PLAINTIFF'S OPPPOSITION PAPERS. THE PLAINTIFF PROVIDES THE COURT WITH A COURTESY COPIE OF HIS OPPOSITION AT THE HEARING. THERE BEING NO APPEARANCE TODAY BY DEFENSE COUNSEL, THE COURT CONTINUES THE HEARING ON DEFENDANT 1405 FRANKLIN 13 LP'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS TO NOVEMBER 16, 2018 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302, LAW & MOTION. THE COURT INSTRUCTS THE CLERK TO TELEPHONE DEFENSE COUNSEL TO NOTIFY DEFENSE COUNSEL OF THE 11/16/18 HEARING DATE. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: SEAN KANE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DONALD FRANKLIN'S MOTION FOR ORDER FOR LEAVE TO FILE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OFF CALENDAR. THE MOTION, WHICH WAS FILED ON MAY 28, 2019, WAS NOT FILED AT LEAST 16 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING, AS REQUIRED BY CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1005(B). PLAINTIFF MAY REFILE AND RENOTICE THE MOTION FOR HEARING. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT 1405 FRANKLIN 13 LP'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS. DEFENDANT 1405 FRANKLIN 13, LLC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS GRANTED. THE MOTION'S HEARING DATE WAS PROPERLY SET. PLAINTIFF CITES NO AUTHORITY FOR HIS NOTION THAT A COMMUNICATION FROM AN INSURANCE COMPANY EFFECTED SERVICE OF SUMMONS. PLAINTIFF'S "SUBSTITUTE SERVICE" ON A UPS STORE'S EMPLOYEE WAS INEFFECTIVE - IT WAS BY AN UNREGISTERED SERVER AT AN ADDRESS WITH NO RELATION TO DEFENDANT. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DONALD FRANKLIN'S MOTION FOR ORDER FOR LEAVE TO FILE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF DONALD A. FRANKLIN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT HE EVER EFFECTED SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT ON ANY OF THE NAMED DEFENDANTS, NONE OF WHICH HAVE FILED RESPONSIVE PLEADINGS. IN ADDITION, HE HAS AGAIN FAILED TO PROVIDE THE COURT WITH A COURTESY COPY OF HIS MOVING PAPERS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Set For Order To Show Cause On Jun-18-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Order For Leave To File 2nd Amended Complaint; Memo Of P&A; Declaration Of Donald A. Franklin
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DONALD FRANKLIN's Motion For Order For Leave To File 2nd Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff Donald A. Franklin's motion for leave to file second amended complaint is denied. Plaintiff has not shown that he ever effected service of the summons and complaint on any of the named defendants, none of which have filed responsive pleadings. In addition, he has again failed to provide the Court with a courtesy copy of his moving papers.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Off Calendar Dec-10-2018 Continued To Feb-27-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant 1405 Franklin 13 Lp'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Nov-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Donald Franklin, 415-794-7904 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Philip Sciranka, Esq. 415-981-0550 of Lubin Olson & Niewiadomski for Defendant 1405 Franklin 13.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant 1405 Franklin 13 Lp'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant 1405 Franklin 13, LLC's motion to quash service of summons is GRANTED. The motion's hearing date was properly set. Plaintiff cites no authority for his notion that a communication from an insurance company effected service of summons. Plaintiff's "substitute service" on a UPS store's employee was ineffective - it was by an unregistered server at an address with no relation to defendant.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

There being no appearance today by defense counsel, the Court continues the hearing on Defendant 1405 Franklin 13 LP'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons to November 16, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion. The Court instructs the clerk to telephone defense counsel to notify defense counsel of the 11/16/18 hearing date. Judge: Richard B.
